title: Winter Spiti: An Ultimate Adventure
story: Winter in Spiti Valley is a magical experience, transforming the rugged landscape into a serene, snow-covered wonderland. From December to February, the valley sees freezing temperatures, often dipping as low as -20°C. Snow blankets the villages, monasteries, and hills, making it a paradise for adventure seekers. Embark on a breathtaking journey where every stop offers a new adventure, from the frozen beauty of Nako Lake to the highest suspension bridge in Asia.
exclusions: Any cost arising due to natural calamities

itinerary.title: Delhi to Shimla
itinerary.desc: We will meet at the boarding point in Delhi - Kashmiri Gate. Leave from Delhi in the evening and start an overnight journey to Shimla.

itinerary.title: Reach Shimla - Transfer to Sangla
itinerary.desc: Reach Shimla early morning. Transfer to Sangla en route crossing Kufri and Narkanda. Visit Kinnaur Gate. Reach Sangla by evening.

itinerary.title: Chitkul, Nako & Tabo
itinerary.desc: Post breakfast, leave for Chitkul. Later leave for Tabo via Khab Sangam. Explore Nako and Gue Monastery before checking into Tabo.

itinerary.title: Tabo to Kaza via Dhankar
itinerary.desc: Visit Tabo Monastery and Dhankar Monastery. Reach Kaza by the end of the day and check into your homestay.

itinerary.title: Chicham Bridge & Key Monastery
itinerary.desc: Head towards sightseeings in Spiti. Visit the iconic Key Monastery and the highest suspension bridge in Asia - Chicham Bridge.

itinerary.title: Hikkim, Komic, Langza
itinerary.desc: Visit the highest post office in the world in Hikkim. Head towards the highest village in the world (Komic) and the fossil village (Langza).

itinerary.title: Move towards Kalpa
itinerary.desc: Pack your bags and depart for Kalpa. Reach Kalpa and check into your hotel. Rest and overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Kalpa - Suicide Point - Shimla
itinerary.desc: Head towards suicide point and then transfer to Shimla. Reach Shimla by evening and depart for Delhi with beautiful memories.

title: Char Dham Yatra
story: This is a comprehensive spiritual expedition covering all four sacred shrines of Dev Bhoomi Uttarakhand. Travelers embark on a long journey starting with purifying dips in hot springs at Jamnabai Kund and Gangnani. The experience involves trekking to Yamunotri and Kedarnath, witnessing the confluence of rivers at Devprayag, and visiting ancient shrines. The pilgrimage concludes with a blend of devotion and adventure.

itinerary.title: Delhi to Barkot
itinerary.desc: Our journey begins at 9:30 PM from Akshardham Metro Station in Delhi. Prepare for an overnight drive to Barkot.

itinerary.title: Reach Barkot via Mussoorie
itinerary.desc: Reach Mussoorie in the morning, experience Kempty Falls if time allows. Continue to Barkot and settle into your hotel.

itinerary.title: Yamunotri Dham
itinerary.desc: Drive to Jan Ki Chatti. Trek 6km to Yamunotri Temple. Pay homage to Mother Yamuna and visit Surya Kund hot spring.

itinerary.title: Barkot to Uttarkashi
itinerary.desc: Journey to Uttarkashi. Visit the ancient Vishwanath Temple and the Shakti Temple. Enjoy the serene surroundings.

itinerary.title: Gangotri Dham
itinerary.desc: Stop at Gangnani for a dip in Garam Kund. Travel through Harshil valley to Shree Gangotri. Take a holy dip in the Bhagirathi.

itinerary.title: Uttarkashi to Guptkashi
itinerary.desc: Travel to Guptkashi/Sonprayag. Witness the confluence of Alaknanda and Bhagirathi at Devprayag.

itinerary.title: Trek to Kedarnath
itinerary.desc: Start at 2 AM. Reach Gaurikund and begin the 22km trek (8-10 hours). Arrive at Kedarnath and settle into your accommodation.

itinerary.title: Kedarnath to Chopta
itinerary.desc: Morning visit to the temple. Trek back to Gaurikund and drive to Chopta. Explore lush forests and panoramic views.

itinerary.title: Chopta to Badrinath
itinerary.desc: Travel to Badrinath. Dip in Tapt Kund and visit the renowned temple of Lord Badri Vishal. Continue to Joshimath for the night.

itinerary.title: Badrinath to Rudraprayag
itinerary.desc: Explore more of the area before proceeding to Rudraprayag. Check-in and relax in the serene environment.

itinerary.title: Rudraprayag to Rishikesh
itinerary.desc: Reach Rishikesh mid-morning. Optional activities like River Rafting or exploring Ram Jhula. Depart for Delhi at 5 PM.

title: Do Dham Yatra
story: Designed for devotees focusing on the two major Himalayan shrines, this journey takes travelers through the Garhwal Himalayas. The core experience is the 22 km physical trek from Gaurikund to the 3,583-meter high Kedarnath Temple. After spending a night near the temple, the journey continues to Badrinath to immerse in the healing waters of Tapt Kund and seek blessings from Lord Badri Vishal. It is a focused, intense spiritual retreat.

itinerary.title: Delhi To Guptkashi/Phata
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey from Delhi overnight towards the peaceful foothills of the Himalayas.

itinerary.title: Reach Guptakashi/Phata
itinerary.desc: Arrive in Guptkashi, settle into your hotel, relax and enjoy the scenic beauty before resting for the trek.

itinerary.title: Trek to Kedarnath
itinerary.desc: Start at 2 AM. Drive to Gaurikund and begin the 22km trek. Settle into accommodation near the temple.

itinerary.title: Kedarnath to Phata
itinerary.desc: Morning darshan at the temple, followed by a trek back down to Gaurikund and return to Phata/Chopta for the night.

itinerary.title: Phata to Badrinath
itinerary.desc: Drive to Badrinath. Take a healing dip in Tapt Kund and visit Lord Badri Vishal. Proceed to Joshimath.

itinerary.title: Badrinath to Delhi
itinerary.desc: Depart from Joshimath to Rishikesh. Explore Ram Jhula if time permits, then proceed back to Delhi.

title: Kedarnath Expedition
story: A short, highly focused pilgrimage dedicated entirely to Lord Shiva. Travelers drive from Delhi to the peaceful foothills of Guptkashi before undertaking the challenging 22km trek to Kedarnath. The narrative centers on waking up early to witness the serene beauty of the temple amidst the snowy mountains and green meadows, followed by a descent to Sonprayag and a relaxing conclusion in Rishikesh.

itinerary.title: Delhi To Guptkashi/Phata
itinerary.desc: Overnight journey from Delhi to the holy town of Guptkashi.

itinerary.title: Reach Guptakashi/Phata
itinerary.desc: Arrive, check into the hotel, and rest up for the big trek the following day. Enjoy the confluence views of rivers en route.

itinerary.title: Trek to Kedarnath
itinerary.desc: Early morning start. Reach Gaurikund and trek 22 km to Kedarnath. Stay overnight near the holy shrine.

itinerary.title: Kedarnath to Phata/Guptkashi
itinerary.desc: Morning Darshan at the temple, trek down to Gaurikund, and return to Guptkashi/Sonprayag for rest.

itinerary.title: Guptkashi to Rishikesh & Delhi
itinerary.desc: Drive down to Rishikesh. Spend some time by the Ganges before returning to Delhi.

title: The Great Kinnaur Kailash
story: An intense trekking adventure into the heart of Himachal Pradesh. The journey tests endurance, requiring travelers to camp in the wild at Ganesh Park. The climax of the story is a 2:00 AM midnight trek to the Kinnaur Kailash Shivling to witness the sunrise over the Kinner Kailash peak. This package is built around physical challenge and extreme mountain exploration.

itinerary.title: Shimla to Tangling Village
itinerary.desc: Pick up from Shimla MCD Parking by 7:00 AM. Visit Kinnaur Gate. Reach Tangling Village after a 7-8 hour drive and rest at a homestay.

itinerary.title: Trek to Ganesh Park
itinerary.desc: Start 12km trek from Tangling village to Ganesh Park. Check-in at camps, relax, and attend the briefing for the midnight summit push.

itinerary.title: Kinnaur Kailash Shivling Summit
itinerary.desc: Wake up call at 2 AM. Start the 6km trek to the Shivling. Reach by 12 PM to enjoy views of Kinner Kailash peak. Trek back down to Ganesh Park.

itinerary.title: Trek Down & Return to Shimla
itinerary.desc: Trek down from Ganesh Park to Tangling Village. Collect luggage and depart for Shimla, arriving in 6-7 hours.

title: Mt. Manimahesh Kailash
story: This trip blends local culture with high-altitude trekking. Arriving in Bharmour, travelers explore the ancient 84 Temple before starting the 14 km ascent from Hadsar to Manimahesh Lake. The defining moment of this journey is waking up early for the 'Mani Darshan' (a blink of light at Kailash) and taking a holy bath in the freezing Shiv Kund before descending.

itinerary.title: Departure From Chandigarh
itinerary.desc: Pick up from Chandigarh in the evening. Briefing from the trip leader and overnight journey.

itinerary.title: Bharmour Arrival & Sightseeing
itinerary.desc: Reach Bharmour by morning. Check-in and relax. By noon leave for Bharmani Mata Temple & 84 Temple. Explore local market.

itinerary.title: Trek to Manimahesh Lake
itinerary.desc: Leave for Hadsar (trek starting point). Start 14km trek to Manimahesh Lake. Check-in at camps near Shiv Kund.

itinerary.title: Mani Darshan & Trek Down
itinerary.desc: Wake up early for Mani Darshan at Kailash. Take a bath in holy Shiv Kund. Trek down to Hadsar and return to Bharmour.

itinerary.title: Departure to Chandigarh
itinerary.desc: Wake up, have breakfast, check-out and depart for Chandigarh. Reach after a 9-10 hour journey.

title: Shrikhand Mahadev Kailash
story: A prolonged and arduous multi-day trekking expedition. Travelers progress through stages, trekking from Jaon Village to Thachru, and then to Bheemdwar, setting up camps along the way. The pinnacle is a midnight push to the Shrikhand Mahadev Shivling to catch the sunrise views of the Shrikhand Kailash peak. It is a story of resilience, rugged camping, and profound spiritual achievement.

itinerary.title: Shimla to Jaon Village
itinerary.desc: Pick up from Shimla. Visit Dev Dhank Temple en route. Reach Jaon Village after 7-8 hours and check-in to Homestay.

itinerary.title: Trek to Thachru
itinerary.desc: Start the challenging 15km trek from Jaon Village to Thachru. Reach by evening, check-in at camps and attend next day briefing.

itinerary.title: Trek to Bheemdwar
itinerary.desc: Early morning trek to Bheemdwar (11km). Acclimatize at the high altitude camps and prepare for the midnight summit.

itinerary.title: Summit to Shrikhand Mahadev
itinerary.desc: Wake up at 2 AM. Trek 7km to the Shivling to reach by 12 PM. Enjoy views of the peak, then trek down to Bheemdwar.

itinerary.title: Trek back to Thachru
itinerary.desc: Trek 11km down from Bheemdwar to Thachru. Relax and recover at the campsite.

itinerary.title: Trek to Jaon & Return to Shimla
itinerary.desc: Final descent to Jaon village. Freshen up, collect luggage, and drive back to Shimla.

title: Winter Kashmir
story: A classic winter wonderland experience. The narrative shifts from physical exertion to scenic leisure, taking travelers to snow-covered meadows and frozen landscapes. The itinerary includes hiring a pony to magnificent glaciers in Sonmarg, riding the Gondola cable car over ski slopes in Gulmarg, and experiencing the cultural staple of a Dal Lake Houseboat stay and Shikara ride.

itinerary.title: Delhi to Jammu
itinerary.desc: Depart from Delhi in the evening. Overnight Journey to Jammu via AC Sleeper Bus/Train.

itinerary.title: Jammu to Srinagar
itinerary.desc: Arrive in Jammu and proceed to Srinagar (6 Hours journey). Check in at the hotel and rest.

itinerary.title: Sonmarg Day Trip
itinerary.desc: Drive to Sonmarg, enjoying views of paddy fields and rivers. Hire a pony to ride up to the magnificent Glaciers. Return to Srinagar.

itinerary.title: Gulmarg Day Trip
itinerary.desc: Drive to the 'Meadow of Flowers'. Enjoy snow-covered meadows, ski slopes, and the Gondola Cable car. Catch panoramic views of Nanga Parbat.

itinerary.title: Houseboat Stay & Srinagar Tour
itinerary.desc: Visit Mughal Gardens: Cheshmashahi, Nishat, Shalimar & Shankaracharya temple. Check-in at Houseboat and enjoy an evening Shikara ride.

itinerary.title: Srinagar to Pahalgam
itinerary.desc: Drive via Pampore and Avantipura. Visit Betaab Valley and Chandanwari. Optional horse-riding to Baisaran meadows.

itinerary.title: Pahalgam to Jammu & Delhi
itinerary.desc: After breakfast check out and proceed to Jammu with lots of memories. Depart for Delhi.

title: Spiti Valley
story: From the apple orchards of Sangla to the lunar moonscapes of Kaza, Summer Spiti is a geological wonder frozen in rock and ice. This 7-day expedition takes you along the precipitous Hindustan-Tibet Highway, past ancient Tibetan Buddhist monasteries perched on impossible cliffs, through villages where time stands still. Experience the world's highest post office at Hikkim, the highest motorable village Komic, and the legendary Chitkul — the last inhabited village on the Indo-Tibetan border.

itinerary.title: Delhi to Shimla (Overnight Bus)
itinerary.desc: Board your Volvo AC bus from Kashmiri Gate, Delhi in the evening. Start the overnight journey through Karnal and Ambala toward the hills of Shimla. Arrive early morning.

itinerary.title: Shimla to Sangla (Kinnaur)
itinerary.desc: Freshen up in Shimla and drive toward Kinnaur, crossing Kufri pine forests and Narkanda apple orchards. Pass through the dramatic Kinnaur Gate arch. Descend to the Baspa River valley and check into your hotel in Sangla with views of the Kinner Kailash range.

itinerary.title: Chitkul, Nako & Tabo
itinerary.desc: Morning drive to Chitkul — the last inhabited village on the Indo-China border and the source of the Baspa River. Return to Karcham and ascend steeply to Nako, a remote village perched around a sacred frozen lake. Visit the 1000-year-old monastery and continue to Tabo for the night.

itinerary.title: Tabo to Kaza (via Dhankar)
itinerary.desc: Explore the cave monasteries and ancient murals of Tabo (one of the oldest monasteries in the world). Drive to precariously perched Dhankar Monastery overlooking the confluence of the Spiti and Pin rivers. Continue to Kaza, the district headquarters of Spiti, and set up base.

itinerary.title: Key Monastery & Chicham Bridge
itinerary.desc: Visit the iconic Key Monastery (Ki Gompa) — an 11th-century fortress-monastery dramatically perched on a rocky hill. Drive through Kibber (the world's highest motorable village) and cross the Chicham Bridge — the highest suspension bridge in Asia at 4,450m, spanning a 120m gorge.

itinerary.title: Hikkim, Komic, Langza
itinerary.desc: Begin the day at Hikkim and send a postcard from the world's highest post office at 14,400 feet. Drive to Komic — the highest motorable village on earth — and then to Langza, the Fossil Village, where you'll find a towering Buddha statue surrounded by marine fossils from when the Himalayas were an ancient seabed.

itinerary.title: Kaza to Kalpa
itinerary.desc: Depart Kaza and drive back through Kinnaur. Stop at the enchanting Nako Lake and pick up local handicrafts. Reach Kalpa by evening and check into your hotel. Watch Kinner Kailash peak turn golden at sunset from your window.

itinerary.title: Kalpa – Suicide Point – Shimla – Delhi
itinerary.desc: Early morning visit to the aptly named Suicide Point in Kalpa, where the cliff edge offers a terrifying and breathtaking view of the gorge below. Drive back to Shimla, stop for lunch, and depart for Delhi on the evening Volvo bus, carrying unforgettable memories.

title: Yulla Kanda
story: Yulla Kanda is one of Himachal Pradesh's best-kept secrets — a sacred mountain near Shimla adorned with a stunning Shri Krishna temple at over 11,000 feet. The trail weaves through dense oak and rhododendron forests, streams, and blooming alpine meadows. A perfect blend of spirituality, photography, and Himalayan adventure for weekenders.

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach shimla
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to shimla. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach yulla base
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to yulla base. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach temple
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to temple. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

title: Jibhi Tirthan
story: Hidden in the folds of the Great Himalayan National Park, Jibhi and Tirthan Valley are Himachal Pradesh's best-kept secret. Ancient wooden temples, trout-filled rivers, and forested trails draw those who seek solace away from tourist rush. This 3-day escape covers cascading waterfalls, Jalori Pass views, and the serene Serolsar Lake — a true offbeat Himalayan retreat.

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

title: Jaisalmer
story: Where the Thar Desert meets the magnificence of medieval architecture — Jaisalmer is India's Golden City, rising from the sands like a mirage. This 3-day desert escape includes the iconic Sam Sand Dunes with camel rides at sunset, the breathtaking Jaisalmer Fort (a UNESCO World Heritage Site), ornate Havelis, and a magical night under the stars in a desert camp.

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach Jaisalmer
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Jaisalmer. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach Thar desert
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Thar desert. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach Khuldhar village
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Khuldhar village. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach Jaisalmer camps
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Jaisalmer camps. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

title: Udaipur
story: The Venice of the East, Udaipur is a city of lakes, palaces, and unmatched Rajasthani grandeur. Set against the backdrop of the Aravalli Hills, this romantic city enchants with its shimmering Lake Pichola, the magnificent City Palace complex, and colorful bazaars. A 3-day sojourn here feels like stepping into the pages of a royal fairy tale.

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach udaipur
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to udaipur. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach mount abu
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to mount abu. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

title: Chopta-Tungnath
story: Called the 'Mini Switzerland of India', the Chopta-Tungnath trail is one of Uttarakhand's most rewarding short treks. At 3,680 metres, Tungnath is the highest Shiva temple in the world, and the trek from Chopta's meadows passes through rhododendron forests blooming scarlet in spring. An optional extension to Chandrashila summit at 4,000m offers a sweeping 360° panorama of Nanda Devi, Kedarnath, and Trishul.

itinerary.title: Delhi to Haridwar (Overnight Bus)
itinerary.desc: Board your overnight Volvo bus from Delhi toward Haridwar. Arrive early morning and freshen up.

itinerary.title: Haridwar to Chopta (via Devprayag)
itinerary.desc: Drive from Haridwar through the Garhwal foothills, passing the sacred Devprayag Sangam (confluence of Alaknanda and Bhagirathi). Continue ascending through rhododendron forests to Chopta (2,680m). Check into camps or guesthouses and acclimatize.

itinerary.title: Tungnath Trek + Chandrashila Summit
itinerary.desc: Early morning 4km trek to Tungnath — the world's highest Shiva temple at 3,680m. Snow-covered and serene, the temple is part of the Panch Kedar circuit. Continue 1.5km further to Chandrashila Summit (4,000m) for a sweeping 360° view of Nanda Devi, Kedarnath, Trishul, and Chaukhamba. Descend back to Chopta.

itinerary.title: Chopta to Deoria Tal & Ukhimath
itinerary.desc: Morning drive to Sari village and trek 2.5km to the magical Deoria Tal lake (2,438m) — a mirror-like alpine lake perfectly reflecting the snow peaks of the Chaukhamba massif. Return to Sari and drive to Ukhimath for night stay.

title: Mcleodganj Triund
story: The seat of the Dalai Lama and the spiritual heart of Tibetan Buddhism in exile, McLeodganj is a town that quietly stirs the soul. The Triund trek from here ascends 2,850m along the Dhauladhar ridge, rewarding trekkers with dramatic views of the Kangra Valley stretching to the horizon. Camp under a star-strewn sky, explore Buddhist monasteries, and immerse in Tibetan culture.

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Mcleodganj. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

title: Valley Of Flowers
story: Declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site, the Valley of Flowers in Chamoli, Uttarakhand explodes into a kaleidoscope of colour between July and September. Over 500 species of wildflowers — blue poppies, asters, primulas, and orchids — carpet the valley floor at 3,658m. Combined with the sacred Hemkund Sahib Gurudwara at 4,329m, this trek is one of India's most transcendent journeys.

title: Sar Pass Trek
story: One of YHAI's most legendary circuits, the Sar Pass trek in the Kullu Himalayas takes you through dense forests and open meadows, across snow bridges, and up to a breathtaking high mountain pass at 13,800 feet. Named after the pristine Sar Lake near the summit, this moderate-to-difficult trek is a rite of passage for Himalayan trekkers.

title: Kareri Lake Trek
story: A pristine glacial lake nestled at 2,934m in the foothills of the Dhauladhar range, Kareri Lake is a hidden gem near Dharamshala that few travellers discover. The short but stunning trek passes through oak and pine forests, crossing streams fed by snowmelt, to emerge at a crystalline alpine lake reflecting the snow-draped Dhauladhar peaks above.
exclusions: Transportation from Mcleodganj to Kareri Village

title: Parashar Lake Trek
story: Set at 2,730m in the Mandi district of Himachal Pradesh, Parashar Lake is one of the most mystical sights in the Himalayas — a dark, still lake with a floating island that mysteriously shifts position year to year. The trek up is short but thrilling, rewarding you with jaw-dropping views of Dhauladhar, Pir Panjal, and the Great Himalayan ranges.

title: Har ki Dun Trek
story: Translated as the 'Valley of Gods', Har Ki Dun is a glacially carved cradle valley in Uttarkashi that holds ancient villages, Norse-mythology connections, and alpine meadows ringed by 6000m peaks. The trek follows the Tons River through pine and oak forests to the base of the Swargarohini range — the mythical stairway to heaven described in the Mahabharata.

title: Hampta Pass Trek
story: The Hampta Pass trek is one of the most dramatic crossings in the Indian Himalayas — transitioning from the lush green Kullu Valley on one side to the barren, arid moonscapes of Lahaul Valley on the other, all in a single day. Camping beside glacial lakes, crossing snow bridges over roaring streams, and topping out at 14,100 feet makes this a truly epic Himalayan adventure.

title: Bhrigu Lake Trek
story: Perched at 14,100 feet above Manali, Bhrigu Lake is steeped in myth — it is said the ancient sage Bhrigu meditated here and that the lake never completely freezes despite temperatures far below zero. The trek requires no technical expertise but rewards handsomely with meadows of wildflowers and a surreal lake reflecting the Dhauladhar peaks.

title: Brahmatal Trek
story: The Brahmatal trek in Chamoli, Uttarakhand is a winter trekker's dream — a journey through frozen forests and snow-covered meadows to a high-altitude lake that reflects Mt. Trishul and Nanda Ghunti. Named after the sage Brahma who is believed to have meditated here, the trail passes frozen lake meadows and ghost-quiet pine forests in magical winter silence.

title: Nag Tibba Trek
story: The highest peak in the Tehri-Garhwal region at 3,022m, Nag Tibba is the perfect introductory Himalayan weekend trek from Delhi. The forested trail climbs through rhododendron and oak to open ridges with sweeping views of Bandarpunch, Kedarnath, Gangotri, and Chaukhamba peaks. Simple, stunning, and deeply rewarding for first-time Himalayan trekkers.

title: Gaumukh Tapovan Trek
story: At the source of the River Ganga lies Gaumukh — the 'Cow's Mouth' glacier terminus that gives birth to the holy Bhagirathi river. Beyond it lies Tapovan, a flowering meadow at 4,463m encircled by some of the most dramatic peaks in the Gangotri Group, including Shivling (6,543m), Meru (6,660m), and Bhagirathi Peaks. This trek is both a geological wonder and a profound spiritual pilgrimage.

title: Beas Kund Trek
story: Hidden at the foot of the Solang Nala near Manali, Beas Kund is the glacial source of the mighty Beas River — a sacred site and stunning alpine lake at 3,700m. The trail from Dhundi camp follows a glacier-carved valley through rocky moraines and snowfields, offering close-up views of Friendship Peak, Lady of Keylong, and Hanuman Tibba.

itinerary.title: Departure from Manali
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Manali. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

title: Bunbuni Pass Trek
story: The Bunbuni Pass trek in Parvati Valley connects the popular Kheerganga trail to the remote Kalgha meadow viewpoint. A short but exhilarating high-pass crossing at 3,800m, the trail offers solitude, forest walks through silver firs, and panoramic Himalayan ridge views rarely seen by mainstream trekkers.

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

itinerary.title: Reach Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

title: Manali and Kasol
story: This back-to-back mountain town combo packs twin personalities into one trip — Manali's adventure and snow culture paired with Kasol's laid-back riverside vibe. From the Rohtang Pass snowfields and Sissu waterfalls to the pine-shaded banks of the Parvati River and the quirky cafés of Kasol, this is Himachal Pradesh's most celebrated backpacker circuit.

itinerary.title: Delhi to Manali (Overnight Bus)
itinerary.desc: Depart from Delhi in the evening aboard a Volvo AC bus. Overnight journey through Chandigarh and the lower hills.

itinerary.title: Reach Manali – Hotel Check-in
itinerary.desc: Arrive in Manali by morning. Freshen up and check into your hotel. Evening stroll on Mall Road, visit Hadimba Devi Temple and the old Manu Temple. Dinner and overnight stay.

itinerary.title: Sissu, Rohtang Pass & Snow Point
itinerary.desc: Drive through the Atal Tunnel to Sissu — a pristine meadow with waterfalls and mountain views on the other side of the Rohtang Pass. Visit Atal Setu and proceed to the Rohtang Snow Point for snow play and panoramic Himalayan views. Return to Manali.

itinerary.title: Manali to Kasol
itinerary.desc: After breakfast, depart for Kasol via Kullu and the Beas Valley. Arrive in Kasol — the tiny backpacker village on the Parvati River — and check into your camp or guesthouse. Evening walk along the river. Try Israeli café food, a Kasol signature.

itinerary.title: Kheerganga Trek (Optional) or Leisure at Kasol
itinerary.desc: Optional full-day trek to Kheerganga — 12km through dense forests to a natural hot spring meadow at 2,960m. Non-trekkers can explore Manikaran Sahib Gurudwara (famous for its hot spring langar), local markets, and riverside cafés.

title: Bali Pass Trek
story: One of the most technically demanding trails in Uttarkashi, the Bali Pass trek crosses a high mountain pass connecting the Har Ki Dun Valley to Yamunotri — two of the Garhwal Himalaya's most sacred regions. The trail traverses some of the most remote and scenic terrain in the Indian Himalayas, including the sacred Ruinsara Tal glacial lake and the rarely trekked upper reaches of the Tons Valley.

itinerary.title: Departure from Delhi – Dehradun – Uttarkashi – Sankri – Taluka – Seema – Devsu Bugyal – Ruinsara Tal – Bali Pass – Yamunotri – Janki Chatti – Dehradun – Delhi
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Delhi – Dehradun – Uttarkashi – Sankri – Taluka – Seema – Devsu Bugyal – Ruinsara Tal – Bali Pass – Yamunotri – Janki Chatti – Dehradun – Delhi. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.

title: Buran Ghati Trek
story: The Buran Ghati Pass at 15,000 feet is famous for its exhilarating rappel descent on the far side — a near-vertical snow wall that must be descended by rope. The route from Shimla traverses the enchanting Chandranahan Lake surrounded by colossal peaks, through beautiful meadows of Litham and Dayara before the adrenaline-pumping crossing that makes this trek truly legendary.

itinerary.title: Departure from Shimla – Janglik – Dayara – Litham – Chandranahan – Buran Ghati – Barua – Shimla
itinerary.desc: Begin your journey to Shimla – Janglik – Dayara – Litham – Chandranahan – Buran Ghati – Barua – Shimla. The drive winds through spectacular Himalayan scenery with opportunities to stop at viewpoints. Settle in and explore the local surroundings as the evening draws in.
